title: Support using wildcards for credHelpers
body:
I'd like to be able to have a single  entry like:

```json
"credHelpers": {
   "*.dkr.ecr.*.amazonaws.com": "ecr-login"
}
```

Or even just being able to use `"*.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"` would be good enough. 

See also https://github.com/docker/cli/issues/2928 .

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Yes, there are some use cases like that (to cross-link : https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-3564 ).

On the specifics:
- I think it’s _bad_ when different tools interpret “the same” format differently. We already have that for the `auths` entries (with Docker, c/image, and Kubernetes, all having different semantics), and it shows up as unwanted surprises from time to time. So we’d probably want a different top-level namespace …
- … and, anyway, the Podman-maintained `auth.json` is not stored persistently. So, the primary feature would need to be in `registries.conf`, as an alternative to the `credential-helpers` field there.

> the Podman-maintained auth.json is not stored persistently

~/.config/containers/auth.json is stored persistently

> So, the primary feature would need to be in registries.conf, as an alternative to the credential-helpers field there

Yeah, that actually feels like a better solution to me. Have some way to specify registry-specific credential helpers in registries.conf.

Maybe add a `credential-helper` key to to the `[[registry]]` items?
